title: Ambiguity-Window Specialist Trained On Hard Negatives
summary: Most negatives are trivial early-stint or impossible-to-pit laps, so AUC can plateau while the model still misses the subtle boundary cases.
rationale: Most negatives are trivial early-stint or impossible-to-pit laps, so AUC can plateau while the model still misses the subtle boundary cases. XGBoost documentation explicitly highlights class-imbalance weighting, and F1 sources emphasize that the real decision zone is the pit window where undercut/overcut economics become active. A second-stage specialist should focus only on ambiguous laps near likely pit windows.
implementation_hint: Define an ambiguity subset using OOF-safe rules such as `CurrentTyreCanReachFinish` near zero margin, within a track-template window, recent deterioration spike, or unresolved dry-compound debt. Train a separate boosted model on that subset with higher positive weighting (`scale_pos_weight` or sample weights), then blend its OOF logits with the global model only for rows that enter the ambiguity gate.
expected_effect: Often lifts AUC by spending model capacity on the small slice that decides leaderboard movement instead of relearning obvious negatives.
risk: If the gate is too narrow, recall drops; if too broad, the specialist collapses back into the base model.
